seo.title: AI Product Photography Generator - Ecommerce Product Images | OpenCreator

agencies.title: Agencies & Studios

q1.question: Do I need professional studio photos to start, or are phone shots enough?
q1.answer: You do not need a full studio setup to get value from this workflow. Clear phone shots on a neutral background, simple 3D renders, or supplier images are usually enough. The workflow can handle background removal, basic lighting correction, and scene generation. However, if the source image is very blurry, heavily compressed, or the product is partially cut off, those issues will carry through – AI cannot perfectly recover details that are not there.

q2.question: How do I keep product images consistent across my website, marketplaces, and ads?
q2.answer: Treat this solution as a central system for your product visuals. Set up a small set of workflows and templates for each channel (PDP hero, marketplace white background, lifestyle ads), then reuse them across SKUs. Instead of re-briefing a designer each time, you just swap in new product inputs while the camera angle, lighting, and background logic stay the same, so your catalog feels unified no matter where it appears.

q3.question: How long does it take to generate a full set of images and how are credits used?
q3.answer: For a typical batch of 6–12 images for one SKU, you can usually get results in around 1–2 minutes, depending on the models and resolution you choose. Much larger batches across many SKUs can still take a few minutes overall. Each generation node in the workflow (for example, background replacement, lifestyle scene generation, upscaling) consumes credits when it runs. If you process many SKUs at once, you will see a corresponding spike in credit usage, so it is best to lock in a workflow you like with a small test set before scaling to your entire catalog.

q4.question: What formats and sizes can I export, and can I serve multiple channels from one workflow?
q4.answer: You can export 1:1, 4:5, 16:9, and other common ecommerce aspect ratios, up to 4K resolution. A single workflow can branch into multiple output nodes – for example, one branch for marketplace-ready white-background shots and another for lifestyle or ad creatives. That way you reuse the same product input step but end up with channel-specific versions without repeating work.

q5.question: Can this solution generate video as well, or is it only for still images?
q5.answer: The core of this solution is still images, but some templates can generate short clips, 360° spins, or simple animated ads. For more complex motion, you can pass your best product images into the AI Video solution templates (such as image-to-video) and build a separate video workflow there. Think of this as your base layer for reliable product visuals that other image and video workflows can build on top of.
